description: Revision for networking, cybersecurity, and internet concepts.

topics.summary: DNS translates human-readable domain names into IP addresses so browsers can load websites.

notes.heading: What DNS does
notes.content: DNS stands for Domain Name System. It works like the internet's phonebook by turning names like google.com into IP addresses.

notes.heading: Why DNS matters
notes.content: Without DNS, users would need to remember long IP addresses instead of simple website names.

flashcards.question: What is the main purpose of DNS?
flashcards.answer: To translate domain names into IP addresses.

flashcards.question: Why is DNS useful for normal users?
flashcards.answer: It lets users type memorable domain names instead of numerical IP addresses.

quizQuestions.question: What does DNS translate?
quizQuestions.explanation: DNS maps readable website names to IP addresses.

glossary.definition: A system that translates domain names into IP addresses.

topics.summary: IP addresses identify devices on a network so data can be routed to the correct destination.

notes.heading: IP address purpose
notes.content: An IP address is used to identify a device on a network. It helps packets reach the correct destination.

flashcards.question: What is an IP address used for?
flashcards.answer: Identifying a device on a network.

flashcards.question: What happens if a packet has the wrong destination IP?
flashcards.answer: It may be sent to the wrong device or fail to reach the intended destination.

quizQuestions.question: Which option best describes an IP address?
options: A device identifier on a network
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: A device identifier on a network
quizQuestions.explanation: IP addresses are used so devices can send and receive network traffic.

glossary.definition: A numerical address used to identify a device on a network.
